knimeNode.name: Spark Multi Query(Hyim)
knimeNode.shortDescription: Applies a SQL expression to multiple selected columns by replacing the $columnS placeholder with each target column name.

intro.p: This node applies a single SQL expression template to multiple columns in a Spark DataFrame/RDD. The expression uses the $columnS placeholder, which is dynamically replaced with each selected target column name at execution time.
For example, with the expression string($columnS) and target columns [col1, col2, col3], the node generates:

p.tt: SELECT string(`col1`) AS `col1`, string(`col2`) AS `col2`, string(`col3`) AS `col3`, ... FROM input

intro.p: Non-target columns are passed through unchanged. The expression is executed using Spark SQL.

Keep original columns: When enabled, the original target columns are preserved and the transformed values are added as new columns using the output column pattern. For example, with pattern $columnS_str, column age produces both age (original) and age_str (transformed).

tab.name: Column Selection
tab.option: Select the columns to which the SQL expression will be applied. Each selected column name replaces the $columnS placeholder in the expression. Non-selected columns are kept as-is in the output. Column data types are displayed next to each column name for convenience.

tab.name: Expression & Options
tab.option: Displays a read-only summary of the currently selected target columns with their data types (e.g. "3 column(s): age (Integer), name (String), salary (Double)").

A dropdown of commonly used SQL expression templates. Selecting a template populates the SQL expression field. The dropdown syncs bidirectionally with the expression text area. Available templates include: Cast to String, Cast to Integer/Double, Uppercase, Lowercase, Trim, Replace NULL (empty/zero), Parse Date, and Regex Replace.

The SQL expression template. Use $columnS as a placeholder for the column name. This placeholder is replaced with each target column name. Examples: string($columnS), CAST($columnS AS INT), COALESCE($columnS, 0), UPPER($columnS).

When enabled, the original target columns are preserved in the output, and the transformed values are added as new columns. The output column pattern must differ from $columnS to avoid duplicate names.

The naming pattern for output columns. Must contain $columnS. Default is $columnS (replaces the original column). Use patterns like $columnS_str or $columnS_new to create new column names when keeping originals.

Shows a live preview of the SQL SELECT clause that will be generated based on the current expression, target columns, and options. Updates in real-time as you change the expression, columns, or options.

Validates the SQL expression by executing a test query (LIMIT 5) against the Spark context using all selected target columns. On success, displays a sample of up to 5 output rows. If validation fails, the node identifies which specific columns caused the failure by testing each column individually. The upstream node must be executed for this validation to work.

ports.outPort: Spark DataFrame/RDD with the SQL expression applied to the selected columns.
